task_completion.description: Load the populated places shapefile into QGIS
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'ne_110m_populated_places_simple' in the QGIS Layers panel on the left side of the screen, and point features displayed on the main map canvas.

task_completion.description: Open the Heatmap (Kernel Density Estimation) tool
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a dialog box titled 'Heatmap (Kernel Density Estimation)' open in the QGIS interface.

task_completion.description: Set the radius, weight field, and pixel size parameters
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the Heatmap dialog, check that 'Radius' is set to 5.0, 'Weight from field' is selected as 'pop_max', and 'Pixel size X' and 'Pixel size Y' are both set to 0.5.

task_completion.description: Set the output file path for the generated heatmap
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the Heatmap dialog, check the 'Heatmap' output field at the bottom. It should show the path '/home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/population_heatmap.tif' instead of '[Save to temporary file]'.
Look for a new raster layer (a continuous surface, typically grayscale by default) appearing on the map canvas, and a corresponding new layer in the Layers panel.

task_completion.description: Save the QGIS project to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the QGIS window title bar displaying 'heatmap_project' or 'heatmap_project.qgz', or a 'Save Project' dialog showing the file being saved to '/home/ga/GIS_Data/projects/heatmap_project.qgz'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the QGIS GUI to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with QGIS menus, toolbars, and dialog boxes rather than terminal commands or Python scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the heatmap was actually generated via the tool
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the Heatmap tool dialog being filled out and the 'Run' button being clicked, followed by a log or progress bar, rather than the agent simply loading a pre-existing TIFF file.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use a text editor to create dummy files
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should not be seen opening a text editor or terminal to manually create the .tif or .qgz files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input dataset is 'ne_110m_populated_places_simple.shp' containing 243 global populated places. The weight field 'pop_max' contains population estimates ranging from 500 to 35,676,000. The expected output is a GeoTIFF raster heatmap saved at '/home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/population_heatmap.tif' with a pixel size of 0.5 degrees. The QGIS project must be saved as '/home/ga/GIS_Data/projects/heatmap_project.qgz'.
